title: Terminate Automatically a User Session After a Defined Condition
description: terminate (automatically) a user session after a defined condition

checks.name: Conditions Requiring Session Termination are Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of conditions requiring automatic session termination is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: User Session is Automatically Terminated After Defined Conditions
checks.purpose: Verify that automatic session termination is enforced via screen saver timeout with password requirement, interactive logon machine inactivity limit, and logon hours restrictions where applicable

checks.name: Conditions Requiring Session Termination are Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of conditions requiring automatic session termination is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: User Session is Automatically Terminated After Defined Conditions
checks.purpose: Verify that RDP idle session timeout disconnects and terminates sessions, interactive logon machine inactivity limit is configured, and session time limits are enforced via Group Policy

checks.name: Conditions Requiring Session Termination are Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of conditions requiring automatic session termination is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: User Session is Automatically Terminated After Defined Conditions
checks.purpose: Verify that SSH sessions are terminated via ClientAliveInterval and ClientAliveCountMax, TMOUT is set as readonly for shell sessions, and PAM session limits are configured

checks.name: Conditions Requiring Session Termination are Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of conditions requiring automatic session termination is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: User Session is Automatically Terminated After Defined Conditions
checks.purpose: Verify that SSH sessions are terminated via ClientAliveInterval and ClientAliveCountMax, TMOUT is set as readonly for shell sessions, and PAM session limits are configured

remediation: On Windows, configure automatic session termination via Group Policy under Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Remote Desktop Services > RDP Session Host > Session Time Limits. Set the idle session time limit and disconnected session time limit to 15 minutes. Also configure the Interactive Logon machine inactivity limit under Security Options. On Linux, set TMOUT as a readonly export in /etc/profile to terminate idle shell sessions and configure SSH ClientAliveInterval and ClientAliveCountMax in /etc/ssh/sshd_config. Document the defined conditions that trigger automatic session termination in organizational policy.
